title: podman build too slow on macOS
body:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186563766-b6a7196a-6ed4-40be-a43e-eaafdf881289.png)

It took a long time from the start of the build to the process of seeing the output.

How can I know what's going on in between?

Use Macos

comments.body:

I saw a similar issue before for macOS but I'm not sure, @baude @ashley-cui could you please confirm/recall if this something which was encountered in the past as well on macOS.

@GDdark , Could you `time` the execution of a simpler Containerfile and paste output here, try with the containerfile shared below.
```Containerfile
FROM alpine
RUN echo hello
```

Sure. I execute it in an empty folder.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186570488-41b342df-0027-468b-9f1a-9803e7d971a7.png)

This build takes a short time.

Could it be a problem with my project? I use Node.js.
Could you share you Containerfile of your project ?

@flouthoc sure.

FROM node:16.16.0-alpine3.16

RUN mkdir -p /data/code/particle-node-server
WORKDIR /data/code/particle-node-server

COPY .npmrc /root/.npmrc

COPY package.json /data/code/particle-node-server/package.json
COPY yarn.lock /data/code/particle-node-server/yarn.lock
COPY dist /data/code/particle-node-server/dist
COPY ecosystem.config.js /data/code/particle-node-server/ecosystem.config.js
COPY .env.debug /data/code/particle-node-server/.env.debug

RUN yarn install --production=true
RUN npm install --location=global pm2
RUN pm2 install pm2-logrotate
RUN apk add curl

CMD pm2-runtime ecosystem.config.js --env=$ENVIRONMENT

EXPOSE 3005
So in you first screenshot base image was `localhost/particle-node-server:latest` and here its `FROM node:16.16.0-alpine3.16` could you time with base as `FROM node:16.16.0-alpine3.16` ?

Both are very slow

I started with **node:16.16.0-alpine3.16**
Later, I thought it was a base image problem, so I changed it to the local image and tried it
The results were still slow

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186588129-21a55a65-495b-4c7e-91e9-4fba20cc8063.png)

I found something
It's while I'm waiting for the build output
htop shows that the build process consumes a lot of CPU resources
It's definitely been dealing with something all the time

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186589388-ac6f9d23-1353-40f0-9bd7-50ecef174ea2.png)

I found that this process appears along with the build process
Does it have to do with this?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186590244-b37fd12d-8deb-4578-b9b3-88c9c371119b.png)

I build with node:16 again.（I downloaded the Node:16 image locally before build）
This time it was a little faster, and waiting for output took most of the time
It ends quickly after the appearance of the output, probably because of caching issues

You can run with `podman build --no-cache -t particle-node-server .`  to avoid using cache and then check. I think if other containerfile are working fine and only this one is slow then maybe its just that steps in `Containerfile` are expensive.

Its stuck on starting cause you get complete response from server when its done building, `podman` on `macOS` is not same as `podman` on linux, on `macOS` it uses a client-server model so I think you only get response on your screen when entire build is completed.

I think its just that steps in you Containerfile are expensive and you get final response on screen when entire build is completed

You can try with some other Containerfile which writes files inside container and verify the same
```Dockerfile
FROM alpine
RUN echo hello > hello
```
vs
In this case on `macOS` entire output should be 60s delayed however on `linux` you will see each step on build logs as they are executed.
```Dockerfile
FROM alpine
RUN sleep 60s
```

Could you run two samples above and verify my hypothesis and then we can close this issue if you are convinced.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186599336-44ee2ee4-d7aa-487c-bd1b-7c0ef90c2886.png)

I tried.
Both outputs appear immediately after executing the command
This is not the same as my project

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186600230-d5e2346e-7842-4e2f-a7c0-bb055775f7b5.png)

I have new discoveries
I changed **Dockerfile** very simply
In other folders build is very fast
It runs very slowly under my project

So I don't think it's a problem with dockerfile
I think i got the issue, if i re-call correctly the current context dir is tarred up and copied to remote server, is your project directory too big in size ?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15982894/186601709-9b8cfc13-b785-4790-99c3-9b20decf7894.png)

Yes my entire project size is 4G
But the file I want to copy is very small

You reminded me
I created the .dockerignore
Ignore some large folders
Build gets faster

> You reminded me I created the .dockerignore Ignore some large folders Build gets faster

Since this looks resolved hence closing this issue :), feel free to re-open.
Thanks

> re-call correctly the current context dir is tarred up and copied to remote server

@flouthoc Is this copy using the same subsystem as podman does for volume mounting? The qemu 9p virtfs subsystem. I just opened #16994 where I've seeing 45MB takes 1m30s to copy - maybe this slowness could be more heavily documented as a footgun if it is indeed the same bottleneck issue.
